background: Exercise 17.4 considered a randomized trial of smoking cessation in which treatment with bupropion in combination with a nicotine patch was compared to use of a nicotine patch alone. The treatment group had success in 87 of 245 participants. The control group had successes in 40 of 244 participants.

task.query: Calculate the difference in proportions and a $95 \%$ confidence interval for $p_1- p_2$.
Why is this confidence interval more useful than the hypothesis test results calculated in Exercise 17.4?
task.answer: What useful information do these statistics add...? The size of the effect is estimated. (The hypothesis test merely addressed chance as an explanation for any effect.)
